decisions.briefing: Markets sold off sharply today with NASDAQ down 2.1% and S&P 500 down 1.2%, a clear risk-off signal. No new candidates were screened, so we're holding all six existing positions and letting stop-losses and profit targets handle any exits automatically.

market_education.summary_en: Markets fell broadly today as investors rotated away from risk assets, with the NASDAQ's steeper 2.1% decline signaling tech and growth stocks bore the brunt of the selling. The S&P 500's smaller 1.2% drop suggests more defensive, broadly diversified sectors held up relatively better. Without new bullish catalysts, the prudent move is to preserve capital rather than chase falling prices.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日大市普遍下跌，投資者明顯減持風險資產，納斯達克指數跌幅達2.1%，反映科技及增長股承受較大拋售壓力。標普500指數則僅下跌1.2%，顯示防守性較強、板塊較分散的指數表現相對穩健。在缺乏利好消息帶動下，現階段以保留資金、按兵不動較為審慎。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors broadly sell riskier assets like stocks (especially growth and tech names) and move toward safer holdings or cash. It's a signal to be cautious about opening new positions, since price momentum is working against buyers.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「risk-off」（避險）日子是指投資者普遍拋售股票等高風險資產（尤其是增長股及科技股），轉而持有現金或更穩健的資產。這是一個提示，代表現時不宜貿然開新倉，因為價格走勢對買家不利。
